title: dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. serotina
intro: Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. serotina, also known as the late marsh orchid, is a subspecies of the orchid family characterized by its late-flowering habit and distinctive purple flowers. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza incarnata subsp. serotina include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and digestive issues.
